coreIssue: This 1:1 is likely about whether a Director-level Head of Product still sees a credible promotion path after being passed over.
openingQuestion: What did you take away from the promotion decision, and what would make the path forward feel concrete to you now?
listenFor: whether he names the feedback in his own words or stays vague about what he heard
whether he volunteers a specific example of pulling back from rituals, decisions, or cross-functional forums
if they describe the mentoring program as energising leadership stretch or as work that feels safer than core product leadership
avoid: do not push him to justify his dip in engagement before you understand what meaning he made of the promotion decision
do not treat the mentoring program as obviously positive if it may be substituting for Director-level product scope
goodOutcome: You and James have agreed what Director-level Head of Product scope or behaviour would most credibly reopen his promotion path, and whether the mentoring program supports that or distracts from it.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one concrete example of changed participation in a team ritual and one specific piece of promotion feedback so you can test his read without speaking in generalities.
